observations.recommendation:
To properly classify the affected accounts in the Statement of Financial Position, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare the necessary correcting entries to reclassify the costs of completed projects as cited above to the appropriate Infrastructure Asset accounts;
Provide depreciation to the completed Infrastructure Assets; and
Request the execution of Deeds of Donation to facilitate the transfer of the completed Buildings (Multi-Purpose Halls) in the books of accounts of the recipient barangays.

In order to present fairly all Inventory and Expense accounts in the financial statements we recommended the Municipal Accountant to abide by the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2024-006 and record tangible items which meet the definition and recognition criteria of PPE but with cost below ₱50,000.00 in the books of accounts under the appropriate semi-expendable property account upon purchase and recognize as expense upon issuance to the end-user supported by ICS.

To properly classify the affected accounts, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary correcting entries to close the affected PPE and Accumulated Depreciation accounts to the Prior Period Adjustment account. Further, we recommended the GSO-Designate to replace the previously issued Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) or Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) with ICS as required under Section 3.7.2 of COA Circular No. 2024-006.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough final review of records and confirmations, especially since some liability accounts involve employee remittances which could impact the benefits due to them.  If efforts remain futile, we recommended that she inform the Municipal Mayor in writing regarding the liabilities not substantiated with records. Afterwhich, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor inform the Sangguniang Bayan regarding this matter for the latter to pass a resolution recommending the dropping of these accounts from the books in order to fairly present the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to avail of the guidelines and apply the procedures on the one-time cleansing of dormant receivables provided under COA Circular No. 2023-008 and other relevant circulars pertaining to write-off of receivables, whichever is applicable, for the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to release the RPT shares of the Barangays within the prescribed period of five (5) days after the end of each quarter regardless of the amount as set forth in Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160 to provide additional funds for the Barangays in the implementation of programs/projects/activities that would benefit their constituents. Further, we recommended that the municipal government to consider the use of an Auto Debit Arrangement (ADA) with the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) for the release of the said shares to the bank accounts of the barangays which accordingly, reduces clerical work and the time spent by barangay treasurers in processing such shares.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the HRMO-Designate to prepare the payroll for the grant of Subsistence Allowance in the month succeeding the actual services rendered and the amount of claims based on a rate of ₱50.00 for each day of actual full-time service, or ₱25.00 for each day of actual part-time service as required under Item 8.3 of DBM-DOH JC No. 1, S. 2012 to ascertain the accuracy and validity of said claims.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that surety bonds posted for the release of the retention money are callable on demand as required under Section 6.2 of Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to increase the level of immediate security for the Municipal Government should the contractor fail to fulfill their obligation regarding discovered defects and third- party liabilities.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor, through the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), to require the winning bidder to post the performance security within 10 days from receipt of NOA in order to not cause delays in the implementation of the projects and to ensure that the expected benefits to be derived therefrom are enjoyed by the target beneficiaries. In addition, require the BAC through the BAC Technical Working Group (TWG) to ensure that performance securities are dated, with validity period and callable on demand, thus enable the municipality to determine the accountability period of the contractor, hence enforce performance of its obligation and the potential loss of government funds would be avoided.

In order to promote transparency in the procurement process, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the BAC through its Secretariat to post the NOA in the PhilGEPS and any conspicuous place in the premises of the Procuring Entity within 3 calendar days from the date of its issuance and post the NTP in the PhilGEPS within 15 calendar days from the date of its issuance as required in Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

instruct the BAC to immediately start the procurement process of all the programs/projects funded out of the RA No. 7171 funds which were not yet subjected to public bidding, and
require all concerned Department Heads to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve. Thus, immediate implementation would enable the municipal government to avail of lesser project/program costs considering the escalating prices of all commodities, hence more benefits could be derived from these funds.

We recommended that Management implement the PPAs as planned in order to maximize the utilization of the fund as intended and ultimately achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes in the municipality as provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 and 2020-01 dated March 27, 2020 and DOF-DILG-DBM JMC No. 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Concerned contractors, through the Office of the Municipal Engineer, to prepare and submit SWA before the end of the year which shall serve as the basis of the Municipal Engineer in the conduct of inspection and validation of on-going and completed projects, and for the purpose of proper determination of the percentage of completion to be reported in the Quarterly Report on Government PPAs;

b. Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the CAFOA based on the verified SWA by the Municipal Engineer for the obligations of actual accomplishments of projects; and 

c. Municipal Accountant to cause the preparation of necessary journal entry/entries to correct the above-computed understatement of the affected CIP accounts and Accounts Payable in the amount of P793,490.21, in order to present fairly the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Inquiry and review of records revealed that Management were able to comply with the recommendation.

observations.managementAction:
The Unimplemented

construction of the sanitary landfill, 1st cell and its two components namely, the leachate chamber and the leachate pond had already started. However, there are lacking components of the landfill such as motor pool, sorting facility, guard house, office, comfort room and water supply.

The municipality stopped the collection of garbage from the different barangays because of the closure of the dumpsite; Every barangay was required to have its own Material Recovery Facility (MRF) and that every household was required to dig compost pit for its bio-degradable waste disposal. Some barangays and households also used recyclable wastes as ornaments to improve and beautify their parks/homes. Garbage burning is resorted to by some households since the LGU stopped the garbage collection program. The municipality required every barangay of have its own Residual Containment Area (RCA) as temporary disposal of residual wastes.

After the closure of the dumpsite, the municipal garbage truck collects the garbage from the municipal hall, public market and business establishments only which are dumped in the municipal RCA. The plastics and bottles collected are segregated, and The municipality redeveloped the closed controlled dumpsite into municipal MRF where only plastics and bottles are segregated. Moreover, a part of the closed dumpsite was developed into a vermicomposting area and the remaining portion were planted with trees.

We recommended that Management prioritize the creation of the LGU’s official website.  While the website is not yet operational, the Municipal Mayor may consider submitting a written notice to the Speaker of the House of Representatives, President of the Senate of the Philippines and the Chairpersons of the House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance about the non-posting of the Reports on the Utilization and Status of PPA Implementation of the BGCM in the website and the reason therefor.  Once the agency website is operational, require the Local Finance Committee to post the required reports at the LGU’s website in order to promote transparency and easier access to information, enabling anybody to monitor and evaluate the LGU’s response and measures to combat the COVID-19 emergency.

We recommended that henceforth, Management ensure the preparation of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and submit the same to NDRRMC, through the OCD and copy furnished the COA Auditor within ten (10) days after the end of each quarter to enable proper monitoring and evaluation of donations received by the Municipality.

We recommended that the municipality fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and the pertinent provisions of Republic Act. No. 9003 in order to safeguard public health from substantial present and potential hazardous wastes and attain a safe, clean and green environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, who is also the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman to conduct detailed engineering investigations including right of way acquisitions before the conduct of bidding and award of contract for infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Management require the BAC to follow strictly the provisions of Sections 17.4, 34.3(ii) and 34.3(iii) of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order to attain competitive bidding and to guarantee that the programs and projects in the municipality may not be put at risk during the implementation of the infrastructure projects and the delivery of goods.

We recommended that Management inform the Auditor in writing on the posting of infrastructure projects billboards within ten days after the award of the contract and comply with the submission of monthly report of on-going and completed contracts as required under COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 20, 2013 in order to promote transparency in the implementation of infrastructure projects and to ensure that the right of the people to information on matters of public concern is addressed.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor require the Supply Officer to notify the Office of the Auditor within twenty (24) hours after acceptance of deliveries pursuant to Section 6.06 of COA Circular No. 95-006 to enable the latter to conduct inspection of procured items to determine whether or not such deliveries conformed with the quantity and specifications in the Purchase Order so that any deficiencies noted will be communicated to management for its timely and appropriate thereon.

We recommended that henceforth, the ‘Requesting Official” portion of the CAFOA and Box A of the disbursement voucher of Special Education Fund be signed by the district supervisor or his authorized representative to enable the said official to monitor disbursements and accomplishments and most importantly, to institute corrective/remedial measures to ensure full attainment of targets.
We recommend that Management fill up immediately the position of LDRRMO in order to achieve a more efficient and effective delivery of services to the constituents.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements are supported with valid and complete documents. Further, management should adhere to Section 9.4 of the RRSA regarding the settlement of suspensions and disallowances. Audit suspensions should be settled within 90 calendar days from receipt of the NS to avoid maturity of these suspensions to disallowances.

We recommended that management require the HRMO Designate to submit immediately the Application/s for Leave showing the corrected balances of vacation and sick leaves of employees who availed leave/s of absences and/or the Travel Order/s of employees who went on official travel/s as presented in Annex “B” in order avoid suspension in audit of the salaries received by them. 

Henceforth, see to it that DTRs of employees showing leave/s of absence and/or on official travel must be supported with duly accomplished Application for Leave and/or Travel Order/s and the DTRs should indicate the kind of leave the employees availed of.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should not process payrolls without complete documentation.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) follow strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in order to attain competitive bidding and to guarantee that the programs and projects in the municipality may not be put at risk during the implementation of the infrastructure projects and the delivery of goods.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee – Technical Working Group conduct validation and checking of the statement of ongoing government and private contracts during the post qualification to have an assurance that the bidders have satisfactory performance in their government or private contracts.  Moreover, in doing so, the capacity of the bidders to perform the scope of works on their ongoing and upcoming projects will not be compromised resulting to suspensions and delays.

We recommended that the municipality as the procuring entity issue suspension orders only to contractors when necessary.  In the preparation of bidding documents and computation of contract time for any project, the topography and weather conditions of the region and the prevalence of typhoons thereat should be considered and sufficient allowances for these factors should be provided in the contract period of the project.  This being the case, requests for suspension of contract time or suspension of work due to unfavorable weather conditions should no longer be entertained.  Also, detailed engineering investigations, surveys and designs should be carried out before the bidding or award of contracts in order that the contract duration will be reasonable and delays in the implementation of the projects will be avoided.

We recommended that the municipality hasten the implementation of BUB projects and be guided with the provisions of DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7.  Also, adjust the books accordingly for the implemented projects which should be recognized to property accounts and make the appropriate entry for the reversal of Due to NGAs account.  Refund any remaining balance of the said liability account upon submission of liquidation reports to participating NGAs.

We recommended that the municipality hasten the implementation of projects under the ADM program in order to have a responsive and timely delivery of services to its constituents in attaining the basic facilities which is the purpose of fund it was created.

We recommended that the programs and projects budgeted under the Special Education Fund should be fully utilized in order to fill the funding gap in the needs of all public schools within the municipality.  Also, please be guided with the provision of Section 5.1.1 of the DEPED, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 for the preparation of SEF Budget.

We recommended that the municipality through the leadership of the Local Chief Executive monitor the full implementation of the programs/projects appropriated under the 20% Development Fund in order to guarantee the optimal utilization of the fund in compliance with Section 5 of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.  Furthermore, those programs and projects described in generic terms should be covered by a sanggunian approval for every specific project to be undertaken.  Also, refrain from charging expenses which are administrative in nature.  Moreover, see to it that the 20% Development Fund should only be utilized for development projects in the form of investment or capital expenditures.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer   submit the approved plans and drawings, statement of work accomplished and inspection report of the Agency Project Monitoring Committee in order to have a basis in the inspection of the completed infrastructure projects implemented by administration.
We recommended that the municipality support the Purchase of Banana Suckers and Vegetable Seeds with project proposal and submit properly accomplished list of recipients.

We recommended that the agency GAD Focal Point person coordinate the preparation of the agency GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) with concerned offices or departments in order to implement all programs and GAD activities identified that will fully address the gender issues for the protection and fulfilment of the rights of women at the socio-cultural, economic and political spheres.

We recommended that the Municipal Government discontinue their practice of settling the suspensions beyond the ninety (90) calendar days from receipt.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should be guided with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the preparation of disbursement vouchers with the supporting documents to avoid suspensions and disallowances.

We have recommended that the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant continue their effort to reconcile the variance of the properties per books and Physical Inventory Report.  A detailed comparison of properties per ledger cards and physical count should be undertaken in order to reconcile any shortage/overage in the physical inventory report.   Also, show documents to support the disposal of unserviceable properties and donations made to other entities or individuals in order to have a basis in dropping off or derecognition in the books of accounts.
